entry.title: Integration btw SAP SuccessFactors to SAP Ariba for user master data flow ..
entry.content:
need to Integrate  Success factors to SAP Ariba for  User master data integration as if the user is created in SF that can automatically be created in Ariba....what architecture we are thinking is  

Success Factors->SFTP->CPI->CIG->Ariba Buying/Sourcing.

Can anyone suggest will this work or not and if there is any other approach for the same requirement. and what actions mapping will be required from CIG, Ariba end for the requires integration.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Webinars | APAC | April 2026 edition
entry.content:
Hello Everyone, 

Wherever you are in your SAP Cloud ALM journey, we’re here to support you. The SAP Cloud ALM Expert-Led Rollout delivers practical, expert-led insights to help you implement faster, optimize effectively, and maximize business value.

Join us in April 2026 for our webinar series across APAC

SAP Cloud ALM Welcome session 

DateRegionLanguageRegistration Link
14th AprilAPACEnglishRegister Now
28th AprilAPACEnglishRegister Now
24th AprilSouth KoreaKoreanRegister Now
27th AprilJapanJapaneseRegister Now

Discover SAP Cloud ALM Webinar

DateRegionLanguageRegistration Link
08th AprilAPACEnglishRegister Now

SAP Cloud ALM Technical & Configuration Deep Dive

13th AprilAPACNew & Noteworthy: SAP Cloud ALM Learning OfferingsEnglishRegister Now
16th AprilAPACUnlocking Greater Value from SAP SuccessFactors with SAP Cloud ALMEnglishRegister Now
23rd AprilAPACAccelerating GROW with SAP project with SAP Cloud ALMEnglishRegister Now
14th AprilSouth KoreaTransitioning to SAP Cloud ALM: Change and Release Management(4/5)KoreanRegister Now
28th AprilSouth KoreaTransitioning to SAP Cloud ALM: Operations and Monitoring(5/5)KoreanRegister Now
07th AprilJapanTransitioning to SAP Cloud ALM: Selective Data Transfer for SAP Cloud ALM(3/5)JapaneseRegister Now
14th AprilJapanTransitioning to SAP Cloud ALM: Change and Release Management(4/5)JapaneseRegister Now
21st AprilJapanTransitioning to SAP Cloud ALM: Operations and Monitoring(5/5) Japanese Register Now

SAP Community Call for ALM APAC

DateRegionLanguageRegistration Link
21st AprilAPACEnglishRegister Now

We have below EGI (Expert Guided Implementation) sessions scheduled in April 2026
1. All you need to know about SAP Cloud ALM

2. Selective Data Transfer (from SAP Solution Manager) to SAP Cloud ALM 

Note: EGI course participation is included in the entitlement of SAP Enterprise Support, SAP Enterprise Support, cloud edition and SAP Product Support for Large Enterprises.

For sessions in Japanese please refer to blog which provides details on registration: link

For sessions in Korean please refer to blog which provides details on registration: link

entry.title: Re: Personas script - Prevent users to reach Main Screen
entry.content:
I think before trying to intercept F7, the combined use of start transaction and URL parameters should be tried first.

Should that not work for some reason, then you could even have an invisible custom control on the first screen and check its presence. To suppress the F7 action, you can use command "return true;". You need to do this tied to the onBeforeRequest screen event, and since that's executed at each screen change or user action, it does have some performance impact, but it shouldn't be too bad.

In general, follow Vladimirs' advice and try to avoid scripts assigned to screen events for best performance.

entry.title: Re: MM Status for "Intl Trade: Import" and "Intl Trade: E...
entry.content:
Hi @Devender_Budhiraja ,
My friend sap does not provide separate authorization statuses for the two International Trade views. In the authorization object M_MATE_STA, both views fall under the same Foreign Trade (FT) status group. That’s why you cannot find individual entries for Import vs. Export — sap treats them as one block. Both are controlled together under the Foreign Trade status. To control them separately, you must use a custom enhancement (user exit/BAdI) and a custom authorization object.

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Week 4 done.

Package ZAC_16013

entry.title: Re: to set STO PO item level incoterm to blank while crea...
entry.content:
Hi @Abhishekramesh ,
My friend here’s the clearest way to handle this in SAP, making the Incoterm at STO PO item level blank during PO creation. Since SAP normally inherits Incoterms from the supplying plant’s vendor master or from configuration, you have to override that behavior. If your goal is simply to manually clear the Incoterm at item level, ensure this setting is enabled SPRO → Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Allow Incoterms at Item Level- Activate item-level Incoterms

Ensure field is optional, not required then in ME21N, go to Item → Delivery tab → Incoterms and delete the value If the field is required in your field selection, SAP will not allow blank.

Hope this will help you

entry.title: Re: Restricting Posing Date in MB51 Report
entry.content:
Hi @Mahil ,
My friend, here’s a clean and reliable way to enforce the “maximum one‑month posting date range” requirement for MB51 (Material Document List) in SAP. This is a classic enhancement scenario because MB51 itself does not provide configuration to restrict date ranges. MB51 uses the selection screen of program RM07DOCS.You can implement a check in one of the available enhancements:

Option 1 — User Exit EXIT_SAPMM07M_001 (Enhancement MBCF0002)
This exit is commonly used for validations related to material documents.

Option 2 — Implicit Enhancement in Program RM07DOCS
You can add a validation right after the selection screen input.

Accept my solution if it work for you.

entry.title: Re: Week 1 : Set up the Basic Trial account enabled with Joule for Developers
entry.content: Package created : ZAC_1605272

entry.title: SAP FI – Document Currency Not Zero but Local Currency Zero After FX Valuation and Manual Adjustment
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing a currency inconsistency issue in SAP FI (New GL) and would appreciate your guidance on the correct SAP-standard resolution.

 

System Setup:

Company Code Currency: EUR

GL: Contract Asset (non-open item managed)

Scenario:

 

During period-end, foreign currency valuation was executed (likely via FAGL_FCV or similar).

 

This resulted in postings where:

 

Local currency (EUR) was updated

 

Document currency for foreign currencies (NOK / HUF) remained zero or not relevant

 

Subsequently, manual adjustment entries were posted (via upload), where:

 

Document currency = 0 (in NOK / HUF)

 

Local currency (EUR) was impacted

 

Current Situation in GL (FAGLL03 view):

 

EUR (Document Currency): +77K

 

Local Currency (EUR): 0

 

NOK and HUF: negative balances contributing to local currency zero

 

So effectively:

 

GL is balanced in local currency

 

But not balanced in document currency (EUR)

 

Understanding So Far:

 

From my analysis:

 

The issue is caused by “local currency only” postings (document currency = 0)

 

This created a mismatch between document and local currency layers

 

My Questions:

 

Is it correct that SAP requires document and local currency consistency for proper clearing?

 

Is the only recommended solution to:

 

Reverse the incorrect NOK/HUF postings (where doc currency = 0)

 

Then repost correctly in EUR?

 

Is there any SAP-supported way to:

 

Adjust document currency (EUR) without reversing prior entries?

 

Or post corrective entries that align both currency views?

 

How does SAP technically allow postings where:

 

Document currency = 0

 

Local currency ≠ 0(e.g., FX valuation or certain uploads)

 

Goal:To bring the GL to:

 

Document currency = 0

 

Local currency = 0

 

No residual balances in NOK/HUF

 

Additional Info:

 

This GL is not open item managed

 

Issue originated from a mix of system FX valuation and manual uploads

 

Any guidance on SAP best practice or similar experience would be highly appreciated.

 

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP Meetup Tbilisi
entry.content:
This year, we are bringing the SAP community back to Tbilisi — and this time in collaboration with New Vision University.

If you work with SAP, want to learn about it, or are simply curious about how global companies run their operations using enterprise technology, this meetup is for you.

Across the world, SAP communities regularly organize informal “Stammtisch”-style gatherings — spaces where people share knowledge, exchange experiences, discuss real projects, and support each other’s growth. Now, we are continuing to build that same spirit in Georgia and further strengthen the local SAP ecosystem together.

This meetup will bring together industry experts, universities, partners, and companies to explore how Georgia can position itself more strongly within the global SAP talent and innovation landscape.

Expect inspiring talks, practical insights, and the opportunity to connect with professionals already working with SAP technologies across different countries and industries.

Whether you are a student, developer, consultant, business professional, or simply curious — join us and become part of shaping the SAP Community Tbilisi.

Registration: Mandatory to attend - https://forms.gle/PeivJnzsT657AuhM7 

Location: New Vision University

When: April 24, 17:30

Beyond the meetup, we are also launching additional initiatives you can already register for:

• April 7 Webinar — “Inside the SAP Ecosystem: Careers, Trends, and What’s Next”
https://forms.gle/xJFhCuipYGXwkrYL8

•  Training Course at New Vision University — Career Launchpad for the SAP Ecosystem
https://gamma.app/docs/Georgia-Career-Launchpad-for-the-SAP-Ecosystem-6tmoxh0jrbnrztv

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 : How to determine if it's called from P...
entry.content:
This is not easy, because you would need to find out whether the current session is running via Personas (either the personas or slipstream service. It could be the webgui service as well, which will also use Personas flavors).

If you look into SM04, there is some indication in the "Application info." column that lets you find out which service an HTTPS type session is using. So based on this, I could imagine that the BAdI code could find out if it runs via something that MIGHT have a flavor active, but there are multiple difficulties with this. First, I don't know how to identify the current session in this list. Also, this doesn't indicate if a flavor is active or not. In other words, I think this would be rather tricky and I don't know how to determine what you're looking for. Perhaps someone else has a better idea 🙂

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 : How to add functionality in table cell
entry.content:
Not sure which 'Overview" table do you mean in PA20, but it doesn't really matter. 

Whether you could use SAPUI5 applet easily or not, depends on your level of expertise with applets. I don't really have any, so cannot tell how easy this would be.

I believe it should be achievable, but this is just a guess on my part. Keep in mind that by using SAPUI5 applets in your flavor, you are restricting yourself to Slipstream only, since this Personas feature is not supported in webgui.

entry.title: G/L Account Short Description showing On Inputs and Outputs
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

In the Transactions are Reports for G/L accounts like ( Manage Journal entries  and Display Line items in General Ledgers )  we found Short Descriptions of Ledger accounts is only showing is there for Input selection and Journal entry display. Is there  any work around to Make those description into long description as a standard to Avoid Collapse of identification between accounts Here i have attached the sample screenshots for your reference Kindly provide a quick solution to meet our requirements

The actual scenarios of internal transactions in our company include both cross-company sales and inter-company transfers, and multiple layers of internal transactions are involved in these processes. Our production model is MTO. Based on these business requirements, may I ask how we can implement the 5W2 Group Evaluation?

entry.title: 🇲🇽 ABAP AI: Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers CodeJam (Mexico City, Mexico)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam session, you will learn how to use ABAP capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers, including predictive code completion, unit test generation for ABAP CDS and ABAP code, and code explanation to speed up your development with ABAP Cloud.

Join us for this day event of developer networking and hands-on learning at the IBM Mexico office - Alfonso Napoles Gandara 3111, Santa Fe, Zedec Santa Fé, Mexico City, CDMX, Mexico. A huge thank you to IBM for hosting this event.

As prerequisites for the event, you need to install ADT in your local machine, you can see the instructions here: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-install-adt.html.

This free, hands-on, in-person event (not virtual) will take place on Monday, May 25th 2026, in Mexico City, Brazil at the IBM Mexico offices.

The instructors for this event are the Developer Advocates @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman .

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page. If you decide not to come, please make sure to unregister using the "No" button in the Will you be attending? box to open up your seat for others.

entry.title: Re: Week 2 : Use Joule Chat to create ABAP RAP Application and explore the Explain feature
entry.content: ZAC_16148

entry.title: 🇲🇽 ABAP AI: Capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers CodeJam (Monterrey, Mexico)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam session, you will learn how to use ABAP capabilities in SAP Joule for Developers, including predictive code completion, unit test generation for ABAP CDS and ABAP code, and code explanation to speed up your development with ABAP Cloud.

Join us for this day event of developer networking and hands-on learning at the SAP Labs Latin America, Avenida Ricardo Margain Zozaya, Zona Santa Engracia, San Pedro Garza García, Nuevo Leon, Mexico.

As prerequisites for the event, you need to install ADT in your local machine, you can see the instructions here: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-install-adt.html.

This free, hands-on, in-person event (not virtual) will take place on Thursday, May 25th 2026, in Monterrey, Mexico at the SAP Labs Latin America offices.

The instructors for this event are the Developer Advocates @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman .

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page. If you decide not to come, please make sure to unregister using the "No" button in the Will you be attending? box to open up your seat for others.

entry.title: 🇲🇽 Build code-based AI Agents on SAP Business Technology Platform (Mexico City, Mexico)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam, you will learn how to build state-of-the-art AI agents using Generative AI Hub, Python and JavaScript. You will also gain the skills to create custom tools for your agents, including leveraging SAP's RPT-1 model. Finally, you will deploy your agents to BTP.

In this CodeJam you can choose to build your agents with Python, and CrewAI or with JavaScript and LangGraph.

The CodeJam will include the following SAP technologies:
- Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
- SAP RPT-1
- Grounding Service
- SAP AI Launchpad
- Business Application Studio (Python, JavaScript)

Exercise Content: https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-code-based-agents

Prerequisites: Chromium-based browser

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Tuesday, May 26, in Mexico City, Mexico 🇲🇽 from 09:30 to 15:30 PM local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

The event is at the IBM Mexico offices -Alfonso Napoles Gandara 3111, Santa Fe, Zedec Santa Fé, Mexico City, CDMX, Mexico. A huge thank you to IBM for hosting this event.

The instructors are Developer Advocates @RichHeilman and @thomas_jung.

Please note that space is limited, as we can only accommodate 30 attendees.

entry.title: 🇲🇽 Build code-based AI Agents on SAP Business Technology Platform (Monterrey, Mexico)
entry.content:
In this CodeJam, you will learn how to build state-of-the-art AI agents using Generative AI Hub, Python and JavaScript. You will also gain the skills to create custom tools for your agents, including leveraging SAP's RPT-1 model. Finally, you will deploy your agents to BTP.

In this CodeJam you can choose to build your agents with Python, and CrewAI or with JavaScript and LangGraph.

The CodeJam will include the following SAP technologies:
- Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
- SAP RPT-1

The event location will be updated shortly.

Please note that space is limited, as we can only accommodate 30 attendees.

Thank you, Yuvi.

entry.title: Time zone conversion for message mapping in Integration Suite
entry.content:
Time zone conversion is not often used in Integration Suite, as date and time are mostly converted to different time zone in target system for most integrations. More recently however, with more standardized and cloud systems being used (e.g S4HANA Cloud), as well as prepackaged API's, time zone conversion has become more common. In addition, most countries use daylight savings time, which requires a different time offset depending on the day and month for date and time.

For SAP PO, the most common way to convert date and time to a different timezone is to use java libraries for Joda-Time in a user defined function to convert date and time to a different timezone. For Integration Suite, these libraries can be used by default in groovyscript. Following an earlier post with an example of how to use these libraries in groovy, as well as some changes to the code to make the function more generic, the code can be used in message mapping to convert date and time to a different timezone.

import java.util.TimeZone;
import org.joda.time.DateTime;
import org.joda.time.DateTimeZone;
import org.joda.time.format.DateTimeFormat;
import org.joda.time.format.DateTimeFormatter;

def String converttimezone(String input, String pattern, String totimezone) {
    
    //set output to empty
    def output = "";

    //if input is not empty, convert input to timezone
    if (input != "") {
        DateTimeFormatter dtformatter = DateTimeFormat.forPattern(pattern);
        DateTime datetime = dtformatter.parseDateTime(input);
        TimeZone timezone = TimeZone.getTimeZone(totimezone);
        output = datetime.withZone(DateTimeZone.forTimeZone(timezone)).toString();
    }
    
    return output;
}

The function uses parameters input, pattern and totimezone to convert a date and time to a different timezone

  • input: date and time with offset or timezone (e.g 2026-03-26T18:00:00+01:00)
  • pattern: format for date and time, using notation for SimpleDateFormat

    Most commonly used notation:

    • y - year
    • M - month
    • d - day
    • H - hour
    • m - minutes
    • s - seconds
    • S - milliseconds
    • Z - time offset (e.g +01:00, +0100)
    • ZZZ - time zone (e.g UTC, Europe/Amsterdam); this can be any identifier for time zone
      • Any text which is not part of the date and time notation can be enclosed in apostrophe (e.g yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss)
  • totimezone: any identifier for time zone (e.g UTC, Europe/Amsterdam)

The output date and time format for the function is always the same, either yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z' (e.g 2026-03-26T18:00:00Z) when converting date and time to UTC, or yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ (e.g 2026-03-26T18:00:00+01:00) for any other time zone. If needed, the standard datetrans function can be used in message mapping to change the date and time to a different format.

Examples

Example 1: convert date and time with offset (e.g 2026-03-26T18:00:00+01:00) to UTC

In this case date and time with offset is used from source message and converted to UTC timezone

  • input: date and time with offset (2026-03-26T18:00:00+01:00)
  • pattern: date and time with offset (yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ)
  • totimezone: UTC

After converting date and time to UTC, the format is changed to remove milliseconds using datetrans function (format yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z' to yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss)

Example 2: convert date and time without offset from timezone CET to timezone UTC

In this case date and time is used without offset and timezone has to be added before converting date and time to different timezone

  • input: date and time, timezone is CET and is added using concat function (e.g 2026-02-25 17:59:23 CET)
  • pattern: date and time with offset (yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss ZZZ)
  • totimezone: timezone is UTC

After converting date and time to UTC, format is changed back to original using datetrans function (format yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z' to yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss)

Example 3: convert date and time from timezone UTC to timezone Europe/Amsterdam

  • input: date and time, timezone is added (e.g 2026-08-27T12:00:00 UTC)
  • pattern: date and time with timezone (yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss ZZZ)
  • totimezone: date and time is set to timezone Europe/Amsterdam

Because offset can be different depending on the day and month for daylight savings time, milliseconds and offset are removed using substring function.

 

References:

  • Timezone conversion for SAP PO: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/time-zone-conversion-in-pi-mapping/ba-p/13156873
  • Earlier example of function to convert date and time to different timezone: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/45834774/groovy-date-timezone-formatting
  • Full list of class SimpleDateFormat, used for date and time notation: http://docs.oracle.com/javase/7/docs/api/java/text/SimpleDateFormat.html
  • List of all timezones: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones

entry.title: Integrating SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance Orders with SAP Field Service Management – Part 1
entry.content:
Introduction

In asset‑intensive industries such as utilities, renewable energy, transportation, and manufacturing, maintenance efficiency plays a decisive role in asset uptime and operational reliability. While SAP S/4HANA Asset Management focuses on planning, structuring, and settling maintenance processes, the actual execution often happens in the field — powered by SAP Field Service Management (FSM).

To avoid inconsistent data, delays, manual work, and visibility gaps, customers increasingly turn to end‑to‑end integration between S/4HANA and FSM. This blog explains the complete process, prerequisites, configuration steps, and execution flow for seamless replication of Maintenance Orders, Activities, Confirmations, Materials, Expenses, and Attachments between the systems.

Key Business Benefits of Integration

  1. Seamless Maintenance Execution: Integration enables a seamless flow from maintenance planning and work order creation in S/4HANA to service execution and confirmation in FSM, ensuring no loss of information across systems.
  2. Real-Time Asset Visibility: Field technicians receive accurate asset master data, functional locations, equipment history, and task instructions directly on their mobile devices, while updates from the field are synchronized back to S/4HANA in real time.
  3. Faster Response and Reduced Downtime: By combining asset intelligence with intelligent scheduling and dispatching, organizations can react quickly to asset issues, minimizing downtime and improving asset availability.
  4. Improved Technician Productivity: FSM provides optimized scheduling, route planning, and mobile execution capabilities, enabling technicians to complete jobs faster and more accurately.
  5. Accurate Cost and Compliance Tracking: Time, spare parts, and service confirmations captured in FSM are transferred back to S/4HANA Asset Management, ensuring accurate cost posting, maintenance history, and regulatory compliance.

Process Overview: S/4HANA to FSM: 

  • In SAP S/4HANA, when a maintenance order is saved, a corresponding service call and activities are automatically created in SAP Field Service Management based on the system statuses defined in the configuration. One or more system statuses can be configured to determine when a maintenance order should be replicated.
  • When a previously replicated maintenance order is saved again in SAP S/4HANA, the service call and associated activities in SAP Field Service Management are automatically updated based on the current system status of the maintenance order and the configured settings.​
  • All the stock & non-stock components from maintenance order operation are replicated to activity as reserved materials.​
  • When a maintenance order is marked as Technically Completed (TECO) in SAP S/4HANA, the status of the corresponding service call in SAP Field Service Management is automatically updated to Technically Completed, provided the statuses are mapped in the configuration. Additionally, all activities within the service call are set to CLOSED.​

Process Overview: FSM to S/4HANA

  • When a service call with type Maintenance is created in SAP Field Service Management, a corresponding maintenance order is created in SAP S/4HANA.​
  • When a service call with type Maintenance is updated in SAP Field Service Management, the corresponding maintenance order is updated in SAP S/4HANA.​
  • When an activity is created in SAP Field Service Management, a corresponding order operation with status Created is created in the relevant maintenance order in SAP S/4HANA.​
  • When an activity of Service Call with type Maintenance is modified in SAP Field Service Management, the Description, Equipment, Duration, Planned Start and End Dates, and Person Responsible are transferred from the activity in SAP Field Service Management to the maintenance order operation in SAP S/4HANA.​
  • When an activity of Service Call with type Maintenance is being assigned to a technician and released in SAP Field Service Management, the corresponding Person Responsible is updated in the Maintenance Order Operation.​
  • After any time effort, materials (planned and unplanned), or expenses are approved in SAP Field Service Management, corresponding documents such as maintenance order confirmations, goods issue/ receipt documents, and G/L documents are created in SAP S/4HANA.​
  • When an attachment is uploaded to a service call or to an activity, the attachment is transferred to SAP S/4HANA.Service call or activity-level attachment replication is supported for maintenance orders and operations as well.​

Sequence of Operations

Prerequisites

  • The minimum SAP S/4HANA version must be 2023 FPS3.
  • Equipments and functional locations are already replicated to SAP FSM.
  • Material and business partners must already be present in SAP FSM
  • Ensure region code in SAP Field Service Management is manually created in SAP S/4HANA following the required naming convention. See more details.
  • CPI iFlows active and configured

Configurations

This section summarizes the technical setup required across S/4HANA, FSM, and CPI.

1 OAuth Config

Use T-code SOAUTH2_CLIENT to configure or view OAuth 2.0 Client Configuration

  • Click on create

  • Give below values for create
    • OAuth 2.0 Client Profile: FSM_PROFILE
    • Configuration: Custom name for your OAuth profile
    • OAuth 2.0 Client ID: Client ID from the FSM

  • Add token endpoint

  • Switch to ‘Client Authentication’ and select ‘Form Fields’. Edit to enter ‘Client Secret’

  • Save and exit the edit mode.

  • Click on ‘Token’ to test the token service. If Access Token comes up as valid then OAuth set up is working

2 Create RFC Connection

Use T-code SOAUTH2_CLIENT to configure or view OAuth 2.0 Client Configuration

  • Select ‘Create’

  • Give appropriate name and select Type G connection

  • Fill details like description and host name under Technical Settings

  • Under Logon & Security, select the OAuth profile created in the previous step

  • Under Logon & Security, change SSL to Active and choose PSE ID. In current system you can choose ANONYM SSL Client (Anonymous)

  • Save and perform connection test. You should get 200 OK response

3 Configure replication in SPRO

  • Configure Replication Parameters

Enter following values relevant for plant maintenance

Scope

Parameter

Value

GLOBAL

RFC

RFC destination created above

GLOBAL

X-Account-ID

Account ID of FSM

GLOBAL

X-Client-ID

Client ID generated in FSM

GLOBAL

X-Client-Version

0.0.1

GLOBAL

X-Company-ID

Company ID of FSM

MAIN_ORDER

S4_FSM_Company_ID

00001

MAIN_ORDER

X-Create-Or-Update

true

MAIN_ORDER

pathConfirmation

/api/fsm-connector/v1/confirmation/${ActivityId}/approvalitem

MAIN_ORDER

pathServiceRoot

/api/fsm-connector/v1/composite-tree/service-calls/

  • Define Plant Specific Order Types

Maintain plant and order type combination that needs to be replicated

S4 Cmp. ID

Type

Plant

00001

YBA1

1710

00001

PM01

1010

  • Define System Status on which maintenance order needs to be replicated.

S4 Cmp. ID

Type

Plant

00001

I0002

Replicate Object

  • Configure Mapping for Service Call Type Codelist

Maintain mapping between of maintenance order type and FSM service call type

  • Configure Mapping for Status Codelist

Maintain mapping between order status in S/4HANA and FSM

  • Configure Mapping for Priority Codelist

Maintain mapping between order priority in S/4HANA and FSM service call priority.

  • Configure Mapping for Problem Type Codelist

Maintain mapping between activity type of Order in S/4HANA and FSM problem type.

  • Configure Region

Ensure region code in SAP Field Service Management is manually created in SAP S/4HANA following the required naming convention based on following setting.

  • Set Default Business Partner (Customer)

Configure the default business partner that will be used in FSM for service call.

  • Configure Mapping for Expense Types

  • Configure for Expense document type item details

4 Set up Service call Type in FSM

      Set up service call type code in FSM settings and configuration by navigating as below in FSM

            Settings and Configuration -> Planning and Dispatching -> Service call -> Service Call Types

5 Set up Service Regions

Create service regions to be used for maintenance order replication. This set up depends on set up done for ‘Configure Region’ in S/4HANA. You can read more details at Create Regions for Maintenance Order Replication | SAP Help Portal

Typically the code will be in the form of WC_<Workcenter Plant>_<Workcenter at Order>
Please maintain at below path in FSM: Settings and Configuration -> Planning and Dispatching -> General Settings -> Service Regions

6 Configure iflows in CPI

Apart from relevant master data iflows, below iflows needs to be configured specifically for maintenance order integration. These iflows replicate updates and confirmation from FSM to S/4HANA.

  • Route Payload from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
    • Route the Maintenance order updates to below maintenance order related iflows.
    • For this it needs only config addition as below to identify correct service call type related to maintenance order

  • Notify Plant Maintenance Order Update from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
    • Replicates the updates like activity assignment release, change in dates.
  • Replicate Plant Maintenance Call from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
    • Replicates newly created service call from FSM to S/4HANA.
  • Update Maintenance Order for New Activity from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
    • Replicates any newly created maintenance activity in FSM as operation to S/4HANA
  • Replicate Confirmed Maintenance from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S/4HANA
    • Replicates the confirmation approved by back office in FSM

entry.title: deactivate 4G group valuation ledger
entry.content:
We are implementing sap s/4 hana cloud.  DEV-Customizing system is in provision now.  CNAP accounting princple is assigned to leading ledger 0L.  Scope item 5W2 is activated when deploying DEV-Customizing systme. We are not expecting group valuation ledger 4G is auto generated with the activation of 5W2.  Now there is error because of  inconsistency in configuration of ledger.  Pls suggest how to deactivate ledger 4G. If not possible,  what action we should take now . Thank you! 

Error message: 

Configuration settings need to be corrected.
Message no. FINS_ACDOC_CUST201

Diagnosis
There are inconsistencies in the configuration settings for ledgers.

System Response
As long as configuration settings are inconsistent, postings are not allowed to prevent database mismatches.

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 : How to determine if it's called from Personas flavor in BAdI
entry.content: Interesting question. From what I’ve seen, one common approach is to check the context or session details when the BAdI is triggered — sometimes you can identify if it’s coming from a Personas flavor by looking at specific parameters or UI-related indicators passed during execution. Another option could be checking for Personas-specific services or flags in the call stack, depending on how your system is configured. It’s not always super straightforward, but there’s usually some trace you can hook into. I’ve dealt with similar conditional logic in other platforms like phonexa.com, where you need to detect the source of execution to apply different behavior — same idea here, just within SAP’s environment.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial
Integrating SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance Orders with SAP Field Service Management – Part 2
entry.content:
Demo

A renewable energy company operates multiple wind farms. Each wind turbine is managed as an equipment object in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management, with defined maintenance plans and performance thresholds.

A wind turbine’s temperature sensor detects that the temperature is higher than normal operating limits and required maintenance.

1 Creation of PM Notification (IW21)

·       Enter T-code IW21 and enter ‘Y1’ as Notification Type then hit Enter or F8

·       Enter Following details to create PM Notification

Short Text/Description: Turbine generator temperature over limit (Add different name to differentiate)

Functional Location, Equipment, Planner Group, Main WorkCtr, Required Endn Date, Final Due Date.

·       Click on ‘Issue Permit’ or Shift+F7

·       Click on ‘Create’ button next to Order

·       Enter YBA1 as value for Order Type and click continue

·       Under Operations tab, add below details to create first operation.

Operation Short Text: Turbine brush replacement

Work: 2 (or any number of hours)

Duration: 2 (or any number of hours)

·       Under Components tab, add below details to add a reserved material for operation.

Component: TURBINE_BRUSH

Requirement Quantity: 3

Storage: 171A

Plant: 1710

Activity: 10

·       You can release the maintenance order by clicking on Release or Ctrl+F1

·       Save the maintenance order and note the order number

2 Verify Service call in FSM

Navigate to service calls under Planning and Dispatching in FSM and filter with the help of External ID

  • Verify details like Equipment, Activities and reserved material under activity

3   Dispatch the activity to technician

    Navigate to Dispatching Board and filter the service call by using Order number as External ID

  • Drag and drop the activity to technician.

  • Release the assignment by clicking on Release Assignments on top right corner while selecting the dragged activity

  • Once released the activity will become visible on the windows app. Do note the activity number to continue with windows app

4 Work and add efforts on windows efforts

  • If app is already logged and initial sync is completed. Do a sync by clicking on synchronization button on top right

  • Once sync completed, you can search with activity code under Activities

  • Click on Work to change status of activity. Confirm any prompts (Do not use CHECKOUT option when available)
  • Click on Reserved Materials to note the use of material

  • Add Efforts by selecting Create Effort under Journal or by selecting Create Effort from Add menu at the bottom right

 

  • Add work time and select the task then save

     

  • Add Expense by selecting Create Expense under Journal or by selecting Create Effort from Add menu at the bottom right

  • Add expense like meals and save

  • Once the journal entries are added you can initiate the sync

5 Verify Time and Material as back office

  • Navigate to Time and Material Journal on the web app under Planning and Dispatching

  • Filter by either activity or service call code

  • Approve each item separately (This is a limitation on test system. In productive landscape bulk approval is possible). For each item provide approval notes

  • Once approved, confirmations will be visible in S4

6 Validate Goods Issue, confirmation and G/L account posting

  • Go to T-code IW33 and enter the order number to display the order
  • From the display of maintenance order, select Document flow or ctrl+F11

     

  • You can check Goods Issue and confirmation posted

  • You can verify more details by double clicking on GI and confirmation entries

  • To view the G/L posting, you can go to T-code FB02 and select Document list or shift+F8

  • Add below parameters for search

Company code: 1710

Fiscal Year: current year

Document Type: SA

Posting Date: Today

  • The exact document number can be verified in AIF Monitor

T-code: /O/AIF/ERR

Namespace: /FSMPM

Interface Name: CONFIRM_IN

Interface Version: 1

Generic Selection: Today

Status Selection: Select All

  • Verify the G/L posting for the amount posted from FSM as expense

7 Create new activity in FSM and replicate as operation

  • Create a new activity under same service call. Important information to add is as below
    • Subject: Example- Backup Battery replacement
      • Planned Duration: change hours to may be 5
      • Region: WC_1710_TATA_ELE
    • Verify the replication by checking the maintenance order (IW33) under operations. Do verify hours entered in FSM being replicated to S4
    • Release the activity to Technician
    • Add an un-planned material by choosing ‘Create Material’ and searching for Turbine Battery
    • Make sure to use material from warehouse 1710/171A

  • You can also add Effort for service hours and sync
  • Approve the material and effort in web app under Time and Material Journal. Do approve the entries one by one
  • Verify the replicated confirmation from document flow on display of maintenance order (IW33)     

8 Create new service call in FSM and replicate as maintenance order (exercise)

  • Create a new service call in FSM by clicking on ‘Create Service Call’ in service call screen under planning and dispatching
  • Give below details and save to create a service call

Subject: Any subject of choice

Type: Maintenance

Problem Type: Any (You can choose Mechanical)

Responsible: Technician Name

Customer: Default customer

  • Note the created service call code. Close and open the service call to check the updated external ID

  • In S/4, Navigate to display maintenance order (IW33) to verify the order replicated. You can create operations and add components to verify replication further

9 Add attachment from FSM

  • On the service call created in last step, add an attachment from Attachments tab
    • Adding attachment triggers replication, no extra step need to confirm the attachment addition in FSM
  • Verify if attached replicated to maintenance order by verifying under ‘Additional Data’ -> ‘Document Assignments’

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content: week 4 completed - ZAC_2229067

entry.title: Re: Questions : March 2026 Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers in ABAP
entry.content: I have completed my week 4 task, package ZAC_2229067. Have to submit anywhere?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:
Hi @patrick_schad,

Thank you for the blog and the clarifications regarding SAP Build Apps and its plans. While everyone understands and accepts that technology and tools evolve, the abrupt discontinuation of a tool that was heavily promoted as a "beautiful low-code, no-code solution" feels quite harsh. Clients would generally be more accepting if a new and improved tool were released. However, the announcement that there will be no future support for the existing tool—at least for the next three to five years, as is typical for other SAP products—combined with the urgent demand for customers to migrate all existing applications to a new tool, is concerning. I can only imagine how customers will react to such news!

Thanks and regards,  

Raja Sekhar K

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:
@patrick_schad  In this post you have also mentioned about "

  • Learn more about the latest SAP Build offering" in which if i navigate the linked page there is a Section called "Low-code" Explore in which Build apps is still available which is even more confusing.Secondly when i research more about "SAP Build" Pricing in official page "https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/build/pricing.htmlplans" i can still see  Build with Low Code & No code developer Tools included in SAP Build developer License Plan. Now the question is what is actually changing with this, Is it only SAP Build apps won't be available as separate subscription but will come as a Bundled solution of "SAP Build" ?

We are implementing sap s/4 hana cloud.  DEV-Customizing system is in provision now.  CNAP accounting princple is assigned to leading ledger 0L.  Scope item 5W2 is activated when deploying DEV-Customizing systme. We are not expecting group valuation ledger 4G is auto generated with the activation of 5W2.  Now there is error because of  inconsistency in configuration of ledger.  Pls suggest how to deactivate ledger 4G. If not possible,  what action we should take now . Thank you! 

Error message: 

Configuration settings need to be corrected.
Message no. FINS_ACDOC_CUST201

Diagnosis
There are inconsistencies in the configuration settings for ledgers.

System Response
As long as configuration settings are inconsistent, postings are not allowed to prevent database mismatches.

entry.title: Best Approach to Handle 200 KB Data Limit in SAP BPA Processes
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I’m encountering the 200 KB limit issue in SAP BPA when trying to pass more than 100 records between processes. My project requires handling over 500 records at a time. To address this, I implemented an RFC to validate the data, rather than splitting the data into batches of 100 using a for loop in BPA as my team previously did. Is using an RFC the right approach for this scenario, or is there a better way to handle large data volumes in SAP BPA?

entry.title: SAP IBP TS-Based Supply Finite Heuristic - TRATIO
entry.content:
Dear experts,

We can not find any information on SAP IBP Help regarding TRATIO in relation to the SAP IBP TS-Based Supply Finite Heuristic. See TS-Based Supply Heuristic | SAP Help Portal  

We found Note 3022208 - Finite Heuristic not respect the Customer Sourcing Ratio - SAP for Me but it does not mention clearly the situation for the TRATIO attribute.

Can you please clarify whether or not TRATIO (Location Transport Ratio) is taken into account by the SAP IBP TS-Based Supply Finite Heuristic?

entry.title: Re: Best Practice Implementation of the SAP Geographical Enablement Framework - Initial Setup
entry.content:
@shirsendu_roy2 , these Servers are for external cloud and on Prem GIS services I have authorization to access. You would need to know your own systems that you can access content or geospatial services from. Your GIS department should be able to assist with this.

Regards,

Kevin

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Week 4 completed in Package - ZAC_1605272

Throughout the challenges, created an Expense tracker app, Validation added for non-empty category and determination added to Expense date field with the help of Joule. It was a great learning experience.

entry.title: Re: BAPI_PO_CHANGE meaning of X key fields of the X table parameters
entry.content:
In BAPI_PO_CHANGE (e.g. POITEMX):

  • The system uses PO_ITEM to identify rows
  • Uses non-key X fields (e.g. DELETE_IND = 'X') to control updates
  • Does not check PO_ITEMX at all

Therefore, setting PO_ITEMX to 'X', space, or any value produces the same result.

They exist because SAP followed a generic rule:

Every field has a corresponding X field—even for key fields where it’s unnecessary.

Exception:
In more complex structures (e.g. conditions like POCONDX), SAP may build keys dynamically, and X key fields can influence:

  • Record matching
  • Update vs insert behavior

Finally,

  • For item-level tables → ignore X key fields
  • For dynamic structures (conditions/pricing) → they may matter

entry.title: Re: C_IEE2E Practical Exam Under Review → Attempt Closed...
entry.content: Hi! It's been 2 hours, my exam status is still "under review", can you help?

entry.title: Batch Determination Enhancement with Shelf-Life Freshness Control (≥75%)
entry.content:
Hello,

One Requirement related to Batch Determination but freshness control 75 come from customer 

Requirement -

 

The proposed solution in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud will introduce freshness-based batch control at the Sales Order level for Domestic customers. For MT, E-commerce, and CSD channels, batch determination will be mandatory and restricted to only those batches that meet a configurable minimum freshness threshold (default ≥75%), calculated based on the remaining shelf life derived from the batch expiration date and sales order date. This freshness percentage will be maintained at an administrative level for flexibility. The customer channel will be identified via a field in the Business Partner master, which will drive the applicability of this logic. For other channels such as GT, FS, and B2B, standard FIFO-based batch selection will continue without freshness restriction. This solution will apply only to domestic transactions, while export orders and branch transfers remain out of scope.

How can we configure this in SAP S/4 hana Public Cloud

entry.title: Re: Creating Production Order Component Line Items via API
entry.content:
Dear @Yash_Rudraksha,

SAP does not provide a released standard POST/API to create new standard component rows in an existing production order via A_ProductionOrderComponent_4. The released API documentation for this entity covers read, update (PATCH) and delete (DELETE) but SAP does not expose a released create operation for inserting a new component line into an already existing production order

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/0a4262096d2e43258df27c594bdd1468.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/ed408e9ecb144e6eb09e6883d25a7262.html

If the additional lines must exist as part of the standard production order structure the supported Public Cloud approach is to create that structure upstream for example by using Create Planned Order with Components and then Convert to Production Order. SAP documents both released operations and SAP also states that the material components from the planned order are copied into the production order during conversion. If the real requirement is batch-wise execution or consumption handling rather than structurally inserting new standard component rows into the order the better Public Cloud design is to keep the standard order component structure unchanged and manage the batch split in the custom solution and/or confirmation logic. SAP released extensibility in this area is aimed at custom-field handling on order components and goods-movement behavior during confirmation not at creating new standard component items in an existing order through a released API

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/720170b7e5f14cf39c378606c53bb566.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d35113ee62644d3abee1aaec148291d9/9622aec3c7ce4b83a9d00cfb46d72ff0.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/dd313229871540368053d17eacf1e2ea.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/c20b8cfa8000468d86a005ec6d03259f.html

Summary:

SAP no released standard API is currently available to create new production order component lines in an existing order. The supported direction is either to build the component structure before production order creation or to keep the order structure standard and handle the batch-wise logic in the custom application design

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: How to Select All Rows and Execute Custom Actions on a List Report-like Screen
entry.content:
@adhimassetianeg 

The solution presented in this blog is designed to accept single values without range or wildcards. However, you could design the action parameter to accept range tables or even a raw filter string as described in the blog here, and process the filter conditions in the backend.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content: This news is very disappointing. In all official SAP 'Clean Core' learning courses, SAP Build Apps was the main recommended tool for extensions. Now the product is closing, and there is no automatic migration for the frontend. What should we recommend to our clients now? Do we have to tell them to forget about low-code and go back to complex manual coding with CAP? How does this match SAP's original low-code strategy?

entry.title: Re: Setup and Activation Guide: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
@Shubham_Srivastava1 did you cover a scenario where you have "BTP Joule Sub account "Connectivity service" configured in a s differnet Sub-account/Region? "? we have a scenario where we cannot create Joule and IAS in same data centre/region as they are not available in same region. As a result the connectivity service need to be created in same region as IAS [CIS]  region and Joule need to be in a different Sub account. Can you point to full document where this scenario is explained?

Mani

entry.title: How SAP’s New Scheduling Capabilities Are Transforming Cell & Gene Therapy Orchestration
entry.content:
Introduction

The advancement of cell and gene therapy has brought forth unique challenges in orchestrating personalized treatments, requiring seamless coordination between manufacturing, logistics, and clinical operations. SAP’s latest scheduling capabilities are designed to address these complexities, offering a robust solution that aims to streamline the entire therapy process for better patient outcomes.

SAP's cell and gene therapy orchestration solution, version 2511, addresses the complexity of personalized medicine with a scheduling engine that integrates manufacturing, logistics, and clinical timelines.

Precise scheduling is essential in cell and gene therapy, as each patient’s timeline must be tailored. For instance, if a biospecimen is collected on Monday, every subsequent step must be coordinated to ensure readiness for infusion the following week. Version 2511 will significantly benefit the cell and gene therapy community by establishing a foundation for automation and future enhancements.

A New Foundation: Scheduling in Release 2511

1. Intelligent Scheduling Engine

The new engine supports:

  • Soft vs. hard booking (tentative hold vs. confirmed reservation)
  • Rescheduling and cancellation (future expansions)
  • Partial scheduling
  • Calendar-driven capacity
  • Multiple manufacturing steps and multiple plant logic

CGTO now calculates multiple scheduling proposals, automatically factoring:

  • Available manufacturing slots
  • Lead times between milestones
  • Defined flow version steps
  • Material-specific capacity at each plant

This enables treatment centers to request time slot options for specific dates, such as biospecimen pickup, and receive immediate, feasible end-to-end timelines.

2. Soft to Hard Booking Workflow

Release 2511 introduces soft booking, which temporarily reserves a slot until the treatment order is created and approved. If approval is not received within the specified timeframe, the slot is automatically released.

This approach prevents bottlenecks and ensures equitable access for centers competing for limited manufacturing capacity.

3. Manufacturing Slot Management

The new Manage Manufacturing Slots application allows plant managers to:

  • Define and adjust daily slot capacities.
  • Associate slots with specific plants and materials
  • View both soft and hard bookings.
  • Automatically block consecutive days when manufacturing spans multiple days.

The system coordinates multi-step manufacturing across multiple plants, ensuring feasibility throughout the process.

4. Milestones & Lead Times

CGTO can now automatically generate standard therapy milestones such as:

  • Biospecimen pickup & delivery
  • Processing start & end
  • Finished product pickup & delivery

Administrators define:

  • Milestone sequence
  • Lead times between milestones
  • Milestones to include/exclude from scheduling

This logic enables the scheduling engine to simulate a complete patient treatment schedule, from specimen collection to therapy delivery, accounting for all required steps and time intervals.

Looking Forward: Roadmap 2605 and Beyond

SAP's Release 2605 roadmap includes enhancements such as custom milestones, partial scheduling, rescheduling and cancellation, a scheduler application, and external scheduling integration.

Customers will define milestones for adaptable scheduling across therapy models.

1. Partial Scheduling

Centers will be able to schedule specific parts of the therapy process, such as manufacturing or biospecimen collection.

2. Re-scheduling & Cancellation

Additional flexibility will be available through:

  • Automated rescheduling
  • Controlled cancellation flows

3. Scheduler’s Application

A new UI for schedulers will allow:

  • Visual slot management
  • Slot assignment
  • Real-time modifications
  • Exception handling

4. External Scheduling Engine Integration

The initial integration phase will allow CGTO to receive or exchange scheduling information with hospitals, laboratories, and manufacturing systems.

Conclusion: SAP Is Building a True End‑to‑End Scheduling Ecosystem

With Release 2511, SAP delivers essential CGT industry scheduling features. Planned Release 2605 enhancements—custom milestones, rescheduling, scheduler applications, and new integrations—further strengthen SAP CGTO as a leading orchestration engine for individualized therapies.

This advancement extends beyond scheduling by establishing a foundation for a digitally coordinated, patient-centric therapy process. SAP remains committed to delivering a high-quality solution for the field.

entry.title: Re: The Data Ownership dilemma — and how SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products helps resolve It
entry.content:
How is SAP supposed to know exactly what our business processes look like
or what specific data the business unit needs ?
This only works for a specific, standardized area, such as the finance department.
Therefore, all the data products must be adjusted so that the business unit’s questions can be answered.
The adjustment will be handled through SAP, or IT will take care of it (again).

entry.title: Capacity responsible planner group in work center
entry.content: I know that it is currently not possible to configure new capacity responsible in capacity header for work center but in our case no standard entry exists in table and hence we are unable to create any work center. The capacity category is mandatory and once it is entered system directly navigates to header where capacity responsible in mandatory. I was able to do it on customizing tenant but on Test tenant there seems to be no entry available in Table TC27. Attaching screenshot for reference:

entry.title: Re: CAP Initializr: Generate SAP CAP Projects in Seconds

entry.title: How to transport Native mobile app create using BTP SDK for Android
entry.content:
I have create a Mobile App using BTP SDK for Andriod.

Now I want to transport it to PROD subaccount. 
Is there a way, i cannot find any help document ?
Or we can only do it using export/import only ?

entry.title: Re: Setup and Activation Guide: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Thanks for this question.

Here are my finidings : 

When you have BTP Joule configured in one subaccount/region and need to use the Connectivity service from a different subaccount or region, you'll need to establish proper trust relationships and configure destinations to enable secure communication across these boundaries.

Steps for implementation

1. Setup Trust Between Subaccounts

2. Configure Destination in Source Subaccount

3. Cross-Region Authentication Setup

Please have a look at this recently released guide https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf

If the specific scenario is not covered then you can reach out to our product support as we may not have released a specific document for each scenario.

entry.title: 4g group valuation ledger with 5w2
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are implementing sap s/4 hana public cloud for a customer who has operations in China and Malaysia. As for organization structure, it has 2 company codes for Chinese entities and 1 company code for Malaysia entity.

DEV-Customizing system is provisioned.  CNAP accounting principle is assigned to leading ledger 0L.  Scopse item 5W2 is activated in initial scoping when deploying DEV-Customizing system. 4G group valuation ledger is auto generated with the activation of 5W2.  Now there is error because of  inconsistency in configuration of ledger.  Pls suggest how to handle this situation what actions we should take now . Thank you! 

Error message: 

Configuration settings need to be corrected.
Message no. FINS_ACDOC_CUST201

Diagnosis
There are inconsistencies in the configuration settings for ledgers.

System Response
As long as configuration settings are inconsistent, postings are not allowed to prevent database mismatches.

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Hi, Week 4 - Completed, Challenge Completed.

Package: ZAC_154774

entry.title: Re: RAP Event: consume SAP standard event and trigger custom RAP event
entry.content:
Thank you very much for this @hironobu_nakayama, it works perfectly for my usecase (filter WBS element create /change events and add some data to the payload).

Only thing I'd point out is there is a missing IMPORTING keyword in the syntax given on this blog:

CLASS lcl_event_consump DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS:
      consume_changed FOR ENTITY EVENT changed_instances FOR purchaseorder~changed.
ENDCLASS.

Once I added the IMPORTING keyword, it works:

CLASS lcl_event_consump DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS:
      consume_changed FOR ENTITY EVENT IMPORTING changed_instances FOR purchaseorder~changed.
ENDCLASS.

 Nonetheless, great blog that opens so many possibilities for clean core development. Kudos!

entry.title: Re: How to integrate an Adyen terminal into SAP Customer Checkout
entry.content: Unfortunately Adyen require a ridiculous amount of credit card spend per annum for you to qualify to use their terminals!

entry.title: Credit Exposure Mismatch
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have activated credit management in production and were cross checking the Exposure vs Open Receivable value. Here we have identified multiple customers with mismatch. Sharing one sample herewith, request you to review and guide on the resolution.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Credit Exposure Mismatch
entry.content: In fact, we can view the details of the Business Partner's Credit Exposure in the app "Display Credit Exposure," which includes multiple company codes rather than just a single one.

entry.title: 4g group valuation ledger with 5w2
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are implementing sap s/4 hana public cloud for a customer who has operations in China and Malaysia. As for organization structure, it has 2 company codes for Chinese entities and 1 company code for Malaysia entity.

DEV-Customizing system is provisioned.  CNAP accounting principle is assigned to leading ledger 0L.  Scopse item 5W2 is activated in initial scoping when deploying DEV-Customizing system. 4G group valuation ledger is auto generated with the activation of 5W2.  Now there is error because of  inconsistency in configuration of ledger.  Pls suggest how to handle this situation what actions we should take now . Thank you! 

Error message: 

Configuration settings need to be corrected.
Message no. FINS_ACDOC_CUST201

Diagnosis
There are inconsistencies in the configuration settings for ledgers.

System Response
As long as configuration settings are inconsistent, postings are not allowed to prevent database mismatches.

entry.title: Authorization error when changing business function state
entry.content: When trying to change the switch state of business function MM_PUR_PURCHORD_REWORK_WF in the app Display/Change State of Business Function, the system throws an authorization-related error and the state cannot be changed from OFF.

entry.title: Warning Message in Financial Statement Versions App
entry.content:
Hello,

When accessing the Financial Statement Versions app, the following warning message appeared:

 

Warning  “This app is outdated and is going to be removed in an upcoming release. Please switch to the successor configuration activity, Define Financial Statement Versions, in Global Hierarchies.”

 

We would like to know when this app is scheduled to be removed, and by when we need to complete the transition. 

Thank you for your time and assistance.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Development Tools for VS Code: Everything You Need to Know
entry.content: Hi @thomasalexander_ritter , any updates? (possible ETA)...

entry.title: Re: DSD Settlement
entry.content: DSD (Direct Store Delivery) settlement in SAP is the process of reconciling and finalizing financial transactions between the delivery vehicle (sales representative) and the company after goods are delivered directly to retail stores. It ensures that all sales, returns, and payments collected during the route are properly accounted for and posted in SAP. Configuration involves setting up routes, settlement profiles, and integration with FI/SD/MM.

entry.title: Re: Remove or deactivate PP/DS relevance for a large set...
entry.content: For your case (300k materials, one-time deactivation), the most practical approach is a custom ABAP program using BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA, executed in background jobs. MASS can work but may struggle with performance at this scale. BRF+ is great if you already use MDG and need governance.

entry.title: Re: MM Status for "Intl Trade: Import" and "Intl Trade: E...
entry.content: Hello Friend, Thank you for the response. However, the Foreign Trade (FT) status group is not visible in the M_MATE_STA authorization group.

entry.title: Re: Spool is not generating for the parent HU
entry.content: In /SCWM/PACK, output determination for HU printing is triggered by specific HU steps/events (like creation, assignment, or changes). You mentioned that the condition record is maintained for HU step “U” (Update). When you add a child HU, the parent HU is updated, and the system recognizes this as a trigger → spool is generated. When you remove a child HU, the system may not classify this as a relevant “update” event for the parent HU, depending on customizing. As a result, no output is triggered. You will need to adjust output determination customizing so that “removal of child HU” is included as a trigger for parent HU printing.

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Week 4 completed

Package: ZAC_105

entry.title: Re: PU Withdrawn qty exceeded by 1 EA : 700000367 YES1
entry.content:
This error often comes from duplicate posting attempts or extra HU quantity. Even if PO = 1 and GR = 1, if EWM thinks 2 were withdrawn (due to HU content or task confirmation), ERP will reject it.

The quickest diagnostic is to run /SCWM/PRDI for the inbound delivery and compare line item quantities with the PO in ME23N. That will show you where the mismatch lies.

entry.title: Re: How do you restrict access to air freight related inf...
entry.content: Please find below comparison of different approaches

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP OData to Your BPA Excel Forms
entry.content:
Hi Vennilla,
your explanation was clear, easy to understand, and very well done. Great job!

entry.title: Re: Batch Determination Enhancement with Shelf-Life Fresh...
entry.content: You can configure batch search strategies with shelf-life characteristics, link them to customer channel fields in BP master, and enforce a minimum freshness threshold for domestic orders. Other channels continue with FIFO. The flexibility comes from maintaining the threshold administratively.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Fiori launchpad コンテンツ - 基本シナリオを試してみた
entry.content: 👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍

entry.title: Re: How combine different PO's into one delivery using GN...
entry.content:
Yes, the function module GN_DELIVERY_CREATE can create a single inbound delivery that contains items from multiple purchase orders, provided those POs meet the system’s grouping criteria. By default, inbound deliveries are created per PO, but SAP allows delivery combination if customizing and parameters are set correctly.

Inbound deliveries can be combined if:

  • Same vendor (supplier).
  • Same delivery date.
  • Same plant / receiving location.
  • Same shipping conditions.

These checks are controlled in customizing for inbound delivery creation

Maintain a delivery creation profile that allows combining inbound deliveries. Ensure vendor master and PO header data are consistent (same supplier, same incoterms, same plant). Call GN_DELIVERY_CREATE with all relevant PO items in the input structure.

entry.title: Optional Task: Use Joule CDS Unit Test Generation
entry.content:
Thank you to everyone who participated in the ABAP Developer Challenge over the past four weeks and successfully completed all four tasks focused on ABAP AI capabilities. We truly appreciate the enthusiasm and engagement shown throughout the challenge. As a follow-up, we are excited to introduce an additional bonus task.

In Week 2, you enhanced a CDS data definition by implementing simple logic. Building on that work, your next step is to create an ABAP test class to validate the logic within the CDS data definition. For this task, you will utilize another feature of Joule’s ABAP AI capabilities — CDS Test Generation.

Task:

  • Select the CDS data definition that you enhanced in Week 2.
  • Generate an ABAP test class using Joule’s CDS Test Generation feature.
  • Execute the test class and ensure that all generated test cases run successfully.

Please refer to the link below for detailed guidance on how to use this ABAP AI capability.

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/use-cds-test-class-generator?locale=en-US
